title: GarageHUD Work-List — the scoreboard
purpose: The single durable plan of record. Completed work is kept with its evidence (commit + test delta) so it can't fade; open work is kept with explicit completion and testing criteria so skipped work has nowhere to hide. This file is the source of truth for what is done and what is next — not memory, not the commit log alone.

session_protocol._note: Every session reads this file FIRST, before touching code. Follow these steps in order.
steps: 1. Read this whole file. The scoreboard, not recollection, defines the current state.

2. Choose the next item: the first item (top-to-bottom) whose status is 'incomplete' AND whose every depends_on id is 'complete' AND which is not 'blocked'. Honor an explicit user request over this default, but never silently reorder.
3. Set that item's status to 'in_progress' before starting work.
4. Do the work until EVERY string in completion_criteria is objectively met.
5. Run the tests named in testing_criteria (default: the full suite). Work is not done until they pass.
6. Record evidence in result: the test delta (e.g. '339 -> 342, +3'), the commit hash, the date (YYYY-MM-DD), and any deliberate trade-off. Then set status to 'complete'.

7. Never set 'complete' without a recorded, passing test result. Partial or unverified work stays 'incomplete', or 'blocked' with a blocked_reason. No exceptions — 'main is always green' (see CONTRIBUTING.md).
8. If new work is discovered, add it as a new item with status 'incomplete' rather than doing it silently.

status_values.incomplete: Not started, or started and not yet verified complete. The default for all new work.
status_values.blocked: Cannot proceed; blocked_reason states why (e.g. needs real hardware, awaiting owner decision).
status_values.complete: All completion_criteria met and testing_criteria passed, with evidence recorded in result.

conventions.test_command: cd GarageHUDKit && swift build 2>&1 | grep -cE 'error:|warning:'  # must print 0  &&  swift test  # all pass
conventions.green_bar: Zero warnings (-strict-concurrency=complete) and zero failing tests before any commit.
conventions.device_deploy: xcodebuild -project GarageHUD-iOS/GarageHUD.xcodeproj -scheme GarageHUD -configuration Release -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008130-001228121120001C' -allowProvisioningUpdates build ; then xcrun devicectl device install app --device 00008130-001228121120001C <app>
conventions.roles: Vector (AI) does all build/test/install/commit/sync. Tim unlocks the phone and pushes. Never hand Tim multi-step click instructions.

items.title: Investment total reads the honest figure (max of live parts vs documented)
items.why: A frozen build-sheet lump sum silently ignored part-price edits; the first fix overshot so partial pricing understated the total (pricing one wheel could collapse a $25k build to $150).
completion_criteria: totalInvested = max(itemizedPartsCost, documentedTotalInvestment ?? 0)
investmentIsLiveFromParts true only when priced parts meet/exceed the documented figure
documentedReconcileFigure and pricedPartsSoFar drive honest copy across Specs, build-sheet export, and Steward grounding
testing_criteria: VehicleModelTests, GaragePersistenceTests, BuildSheetExporterTests, StewardGroundingTests updated and green

result.notes: Open trade-off tracked as W-008: when documented > priced, the headline holds at documented until parts catch up.

items.title: Coherence pass — honest voice copy, tappable fleet Steward rows, Specs consolidation
items.why: Honesty is the app's pitch; stale 'voice on the way' copy, a fabricated spoken HP figure, read-only Steward rows, and an 8-panel Specs screen all eroded it.
completion_criteria: No fabricated numbers in voice test
Garage Steward rows navigate to the subject car
Specs is one calm Numbers section

items.title: Phase 3 — the Build Plan (intent layer)
items.why: The app knew what a car is and what's off now, but not where it's headed.
completion_criteria: BuildGoal on Vehicle
BuildPlanner orders wishlist parts support/sequence/power
Dashboard panel + goal editor
advisory on boost-without-fueling
testing_criteria: BuildPlanTests cover ordering, sequence tagging, advisory, goal persistence

items.title: Wheel-honest build-goal progress
items.why: Progress compared a crank factory rating to a wheel target, overstating % to goal for un-dynoed cars.
completion_criteria: currentWheelHorsepowerEstimate (latest wheel dyno, else stock wheel baseline; never crank)
BuildProgress and the goal advisory use it
testing_criteria: BuildPlanTests: un-dynoed car with a wheel goal is not falsely 'at goal'

items.title: Shareable sheets export as named files, not bare strings
items.why: Sharing a bare String gave the share sheet no filename, so 'Save to Files' fell back to stale clipboard data.
completion_criteria: SharableTextFile exports a titled .txt and plain text
build sheet and OBD report routed through it

items.title: Export-icon vocabulary — share arrow = sheet, archivebox = backup
items.why: The garage JSON backup wore the same share-arrow glyph as the build sheet, so it read as 'share this car' and handed over raw JSON.
completion_criteria: Both garage-backup exports use archivebox + 'restore file' preview
share arrow reserved for human-readable sheets

items.title: Whole-fleet sheet as a polished PDF
items.why: The only whole-garage export was JSON; owners need a human-readable document to hand a buyer or shop.
completion_criteria: FleetSheet renders the garage from HUDTheme to a single-page PDF
measured-vs-estimated graded
dot legend for recipients
shared from the garage share arrow
failed render throws instead of emitting a 0-byte file
testing_criteria: FleetSheetTests: valid PDF, empty fleet, full 8-bay render within CGContext limits

result.notes: Visually inspected the rendered PDF before shipping.

items.title: Tune the cost-precedence threshold against real cars
items.why: When the documented total exceeds priced parts, the headline holds at documented until parts catch up — deliberate anti-understatement, but it means a single part edit won't move the headline in that window. Confirm this feels right on Tim's actual S2K/Fozzy data, or introduce a coverage threshold.
completion_criteria: Decision recorded: keep max() as-is OR switch to a coverage rule (e.g. parts-live once priced parts cover N% of installed parts)
behavior matches Tim's expectation on his real fleet
testing_criteria: any rule change covered by VehicleModelTests

result.notes: Decision (Tim authorized 'GO'): keep totalInvested = max(itemized, documented). It's correct for Tim's real fleet (documented ≈ itemized, so parts drive it and edits move it), and the one soft spot — when documented > priced, the headline holds at documented until parts catch up — is already made transparent on Specs by the '$X of it priced so far' line. No coverage-threshold refinement needed. Revisit only if a real car's headline ever feels wrong.

items.title: Validate fleet-sheet sharing on a real device
items.why: The PDF render is unit-tested and eyeballed, but AirDrop/Files/Mail behavior, preview thumbnail, and large-fleet feel are unverified on hardware.
completion_criteria: Share the fleet sheet to Files, Mail, and AirDrop on the device; confirm a titled .pdf opens correctly
confirm the share-sheet preview reads well
testing_criteria: manual on-device check; note results here

result.on_device: Tim confirmed 2026-07-16: 'export to pdf works great, just as mocked up.'
verified_on_host: The exact bytes SharableFleetSheet exports (FleetSheetPDF.data) were written to 'GarageHUD Fleet Sheet.pdf' and open: macOS reports com.adobe.pdf and QuickLook renders it.
PDFKit: 1 page, 612x1271pt (US-Letter width, well under the 14,400pt CGContext ceiling), with 1003 chars of SELECTABLE vector text — crisp and searchable, not a rasterized image.
Rendered page reflects current honest logic: documented-total labeling (W-001), the dot legend, and goal %.
result.note: Stale 'remaining_on_device' checklist removed 2026-07-17 — superseded by Tim's on-device confirmation above.

items.title: Surface the first-run 'download a better voice' nudge
items.why: needsBetterVoiceDownload is wired but never shown, so a user with only the default robotic voice isn't guided to the Premium download (TD-007).
completion_criteria: A calm first-run nudge appears when only a default voice is installed and cloud voice is off
dismissible, non-nagging
testing_criteria: logic covered where testable; manual on-device confirm

result.notes: VoiceNudge (pure, tested) gates the nudge: shows only when the device has just the robotic default voice, cloud voice is off, and it hasn't been dismissed; dismissal persists in UserDefaults so it never nags. Surfaced as a calm, dismissible banner in Ask Steward (where the voice is first heard) with a 'Voice settings' button and the iOS enhanced-voice download path. Gating unit-tested; the banner only appears on a device in the robotic-only state, which can't be forced on Tim's phone (his has enhanced voices), so visual appearance is condition-gated by the tested rule rather than eyeballed.

items.title: OBD adapter pairing UI
items.why: Handshake, profile model, and known-peripheral reconnect exist and are tested, but there's no UI to present discovered adapters, pick one, and persist the profile (TD-004).
completion_criteria: Present discovered ELM327 candidates
owner picks one; profile persisted
knownPeripheralID loaded on next launch

result.notes: Reconciled with the other dev's Jul-13 prior art (a6f65ae scan-first picker) instead of my from-scratch abstraction (dropped OBDPairing/MockOBDPairingService). Their architecture — scan-first hooks on OBDLiveDataSource reusing its proven bring-up — is better and I adopted it. Added on main's evolved base: catalog-awareness (OBDAdapterCandidate.isReachableOverBLE/unreachableReason so an MFi MX+ is a shown dead-end, not an inviting tap) and a persistence fix (record impliedSelection on pick so the saved profile is accepted next launch via acceptsSavedProfile). Picker rendered and visually verified. On-device BLE scan/validate is exercised in W-014 (blocked on hardware).

items.title: Version the CloudKit payload
items.why: The local file is a versioned document, but the CloudKit graph is still an unversioned bare array; version it before any non-additive change to the synced schema (TD-005 remaining, relates TD-001).
completion_criteria: CloudKit payload carries a schemaVersion
legacy bare-array payloads migrate in place
testing_criteria: round-trip + migration tests for the synced payload

result.notes: CloudKit payload now uses the same versioned Document {schemaVersion, vehicles} envelope as the local file (CloudSyncManager.encodePayload/decodePayload reuse GaragePersistence). Pull tolerates both the versioned form and a legacy bare array, so older cloud records are never dropped. CloudPayloadTests covers round-trip, legacy tolerance, unreadable->nil. Real CloudKit round-trip cannot be unit-tested; the payload envelope is. One-time transition caveat documented in PERSISTENCE.md; TD-005 remaining now resolved.

items.title: On-device validation: cloud voice, LLM Steward, speech-in
items.why: Cloud TTS, the Foundation Models LLM path, and SFSpeechRecognizer are all gated and tested in logic but never exercised on real hardware/OS (TD-007).
completion_criteria: Cloud voice end-to-end with a real key (latency, audio-session/mic interplay, cost)
LLM path on an Apple-Intelligence device honors confidence bands and refuses out-of-record questions
speech-in works against a real mic
testing_criteria: manual on-device validation; record findings and any TD updates here
items.blocked_reason: Needs an OpenAI API key (Tim enters it directly into the app — Vector never handles keys) AND an Apple-Intelligence device (iPhone 15 Pro+/iOS 26) with a physical mic. Logic is built and gated; only real-hardware/credential validation remains.

items.title: OBD hardware validation
items.why: Real-adapter integration, ISO-TP/multi-ECU headers, and live disconnect/reconnect are unverified against physical hardware (TD-004). Synthetic transcript replay is done.
completion_criteria: Connect to a real ELM327 and decode live PIDs
handle a multi-ECU/ISO-TP response
survive a disconnect/reconnect cycle
items.blocked_reason: Needs a Bluetooth-LE adapter. Tim has an OBDLink MX+, which is MFi/Bluetooth-Classic — iOS pairs it but GarageHUD's CoreBluetooth stack can't open its data channel (no MFi entitlement). The picker (W-011) shows it as a dead-end. Get an OBDLink CX (BLE, same brand) or a Veepeak/Vgate BLE adapter; then W-014 is a plug-and-check.

items.title: DD-001 — eye-gravity review of every major screen
items.why: Design debt: each major screen should be reviewed for where the eye lands first; if two reviewers disagree, the hierarchy isn't finished.
completion_criteria: Each major screen reviewed for first-glance focus
any hierarchy fixes landed or logged as follow-up items
testing_criteria: design review (no automated test); record conclusions here

result.notes: First-reviewer eye-gravity pass: Garage reviewed from a live Simulator screenshot, other screens from code. Findings recorded in docs/DESIGN_DEBT.md (DD-001). Landed F1 (clear fix): the fleet-health strip clipped SERVICE DUE behind a non-obvious horizontal scroll; now wraps to two rows via ViewThatFits, verified on device before/after. F2 (power competes with state on an out-of-service car), F3 (active car shown twice), F4 (top-right bay-tab/arrow crowding), and a Dashboard condition-summary idea are logged for Tim as second reviewer (DD-001 rule: if two reviewers disagree, hierarchy is not finished).

items.title: Model vehicle purchase price (acquisition cost), distinct from build investment
items.why: GarageHUD has no purchase-price field — the only money fields are build/mod spend (documentedTotalInvestment, part costs). For a mostly-stock utility truck like the Tundra, what you paid is the primary money fact and it has nowhere to live, so Tim reasonably put '60K purchase price' in a free-text Note, which never becomes a number. Notes are prose; the figure can't surface.
completion_criteria: Vehicle gains a purchasePrice: Double? (tolerant decode)
Editable on Specs and shown as its own figure (not silently folded into 'Total Invested', which stays build/mod spend)
DECISION PENDING (Tim): whether to also show a combined 'all-in' (purchase + build) line, and whether purchase price appears on the fleet sheet
Honest wording: 'Purchase price' / 'paid', separate from 'invested'
testing_criteria: VehicleModelTests: purchasePrice round-trips and does not alter totalInvested

result.notes: Vehicle.purchasePrice added (tolerant decode, older files -> nil). Shown as its own 'Purchase Price' line under a new OWNERSHIP section on Specs, editable, kept out of totalInvested. Separate lines, no all-in. Fleet-sheet inclusion deferred.

items.decision: Tim chose (2026-07-16): separate lines (no all-in combined line). Fleet-sheet inclusion deferred unless asked.

items.title: Maintenance / service cost tracking
items.why: Service records (oil changes, tire rotations) had no cost field, so lifetime maintenance spend couldn't be tracked — the other half of why Tim's Tundra costs ended up in a note. Requested alongside W-016.
completion_criteria: BuildEvent gains cost: Double? (service occurrence carries what it cost)
A service record's cost is editable in Service History
Vehicle.serviceSpend sums service-record costs; shown as its own line on Specs, separate from Total Invested and Purchase price
Total service spend surfaced in the Service History view
testing_criteria: VehicleModelTests: BuildEvent.cost round-trips; serviceSpend sums; markMaintenanceDone can carry a cost; totalInvested unaffected

result.notes: BuildEvent.cost added; markMaintenanceDone takes an optional cost; setBuildEventCost prices/clears a record after the fact. Editable per record in Service History (tap 'Add cost'), with a running TOTAL SERVICE SPEND header. Vehicle.serviceSpend shown as its own line on Specs, separate from Total Invested and Purchase price. Follow-up (Tim): 'Mark done' now prompts for the cost via an optional alert field — Log works with it blank, so the prompt never restricts the record.

items.title: F2: dim POWER on an out-of-service car (DD-001 eye-gravity)
items.why: On a torn-down car the bright cyan POWER (e.g. 477 WHP) out-competed the amber OUT OF SERVICE and red TUNE STATE for the eye — power isn't the story when the engine is apart.
completion_criteria: Hero instrument rail shows POWER in textPrimary (not cyan) while serviceStatus.isInService
Cyan retained when the car is in service

result.notes: POWER cell in the hero instrument rail now renders textPrimary (plain white) while serviceStatus.isInService, cyan otherwise. Verified in the simulator: on the out-of-service S2K the eye now lands on OUT OF SERVICE / TUNE STATE HOLD / NEXT ACTION rather than the dyno number. F3 (active car shown twice) and F4 (bay-tab/arrow crowding) remain logged in DESIGN_DEBT.md for Tim.

items.title: F3: active car not shown twice on the garage
items.why: The spotlighted (active-bay) car appeared as both the hero and the first grid card below it.
completion_criteria: Grid omits the spotlighted vehicle (LazyVGrid reflows); the fleet map shows the rest of the garage

result.notes: Verified: with S2K spotlighted, the grid now leads with FOZZY, not a second S2K card.

items.title: F4: bay label vs open-arrow collision on the hero
items.why: VehicleVisualFallback rendered 'BAY N' top-right, the exact corner the spotlight overlays its open-arrow button — they overlapped.
completion_criteria: 'BAY N' moved to the top-left of the hero art, clear of the top-right arrow, in both the garage spotlight and the per-car detail hero

result.notes: Verified: BAY 1 now sits top-left, arrow alone top-right, no overlap.

items.title: Honesty audit: a numberless dyno was labeled measured
items.why: Adversarial audit — a .dyno record with no wheelHorsepower made currentHorsepowerEstimate fall back to the CRANK factory figure while 'measured' was gated on record TYPE, so the build sheet and fleet sheet (both shareable) stated e.g. '155 whp measured' for a car that never measured wheel power. Crank shown as wheel, estimate shown as measured. Adjacent to W-004, which fixed only the Build-Plan number and left the label paths leaky.
completion_criteria: measuredWheelHorsepower + hasMeasuredPower on Vehicle (a real dyno number is the only thing that counts as measured)
currentHorsepowerEstimate = measuredWheelHorsepower ?? factory (no crank-via-numberless-dyno, no shadowing an earlier real dyno)
every measured label gated on hasMeasuredPower, not record type (build sheet, fleet sheet, BuildProgress, dashboard torque)
testing_criteria: HonestyInvariantsTests: numberless dyno never labeled measured; earlier real dyno not shadowed; installed part overrides confirmed-stock; planned part not counted as covered

result.notes: Empirically confirmed the lie ('155 whp measured'), fixed, and locked with regression tests. Audit also cleared attacks 2 (installed part overrides stale stock claim) and 3 (planned part reads 'undocumented, planned', not 'covered') as honest. Attack 4 (absurd user-entered dyno reported raw) is garbage-in, not an engine lie — possible future sanity-flag, not tracked. UPDATE 2026-07-17: attack 4 was overruled by the Fable review (W-022) — a non-positive dyno figure no longer counts as a measurement.

items.title: Fable review fixes — six proven honesty lies, three integrity bugs
items.why: Independent adversarial review (Fable, 2026-07-17) empirically proved the app stating falsehoods: crank labeled 'whp' in BuildAssessment.powerSummary (third instance of the W-004/W-021 class); wishlist costs reported as spend on Specs and in LLM grounding; a 3,000-mi-overdue truck producing zero Steward observations ('Nothing stands out right now.') while the briefing header called it overdue; gap evidence denying a logged wishlist part; grounding denying a logged numberless dyno; '-50 whp (measured)' on the shareable build sheet. Plus: a fresh device could clobber the whole cloud garage (conflict guard skipped when appliedCloudUpdatedAt was nil); mark-done with unknown odometer kept a stale mileage baseline (fresh service read overdue); OBD boost hardcoded sea-level baro; moveParts carried the source car's installDate into the destination's history.
completion_criteria: Vehicle.measuredDynoRecords/latestMeasuredDyno is the single source for every measured-power claim; non-positive figures never count
spendByCategory counts installed parts only
Steward.observe judges maintenance mileage-aware, same as every other surface; evidence and build-sheet copy name the actual overdue driver
supportGap acknowledges planned parts; grounding acknowledges numberless dyno sessions and labels power-to-weight basis
pushNow treats never-applied-cloud-state as a conflict (snapshot + adopt) instead of blind-pushing; stripped-remote exception preserved
markMaintenanceDone clears the mileage baseline when the odometer is unknown
OBD decoder reports raw MAP kPa, polls baro PID 0133, computes gauge boost against measured baro
moveParts re-dates the moved part to the transfer moment
testing_criteria: HonestyInvariantsTests regression per lie; PartTransferTests re-date; MileageMaintenanceTests baseline-clear; OBDPIDDecoderTests baro/gauge

result.notes: Remaining from the review, deliberately NOT done here (needs owner/architect judgment): a table-driven cross-surface honesty sweep test (adversarial vehicle matrix x every textual surface x global assertions) — the structural guard against instance #4 of the crank-as-wheel class; a PowerFigure/money-bucket type so value+label can't be mis-paired at call sites; odometer input validation; units/locale; a UI path to discover and restore conflict snapshots. UPDATE: Tim authorized continuing — tracked as W-023..W-026.

items.title: Cross-surface honesty sweep test
items.why: Three reviews found the same value/label mis-pair (crank as 'whp', estimate as 'measured') in three different places — point fixes don't stop instance #4; a quantified invariant does. Designing the sweep immediately caught a fourth live instance: the voice Steward's power answer said 'No dyno is logged yet' for a car with a numberless dyno session on record (the H5 denial, in a surface the grounding fix missed).
completion_criteria: HonestySweepTests renders every pure textual surface (build sheet, grounding, observations, assessment, next step, four voice replies, briefing, tune readiness, build plan) for an adversarial vehicle matrix

Global assertions: whp earned or estimate-framed; 'measured' earned or negated; the record never denied; planned money always framed planned; a bare car yields no numbers; surfaces agree on overdue-ness
Mutation-verified: reintroducing the W-022 crank-as-whp bug makes the sweep fail with a pointed message
StewardConversation numberless-dyno denial fixed (same wording as grounding)

result.tests: 376 -> 377, +1 (one sweep test, ~110 vehicle x surface x rule checks inside)
result.notes: New textual surfaces must be added to HonestySweepTests.surfaces to inherit coverage — noted in the test header. Fleet-sheet PDF is SwiftUI-rendered and not sweepable as text; its logic reads the same shared helpers.

items.title: PowerFigure — value and honest label paired in one type
items.why: The recurring crank-as-whp bug was structural: currentHorsepowerEstimate (which can be a crank rating) and hasMeasuredPower lived apart, and every surface re-paired them by hand. Wiring the type up found FOUR MORE live view-layer instances the text sweep can't reach: VehicleIdentitySurface, GarageSpotlightView (the garage hero rail printed a crank rating as 'WHP'), GarageOverviewView, and FleetComparisonView (crank and wheel figures mixed in one column under a shared 'whp' header).
completion_criteria: PowerFigure { value, isMeasured, unit, qualifier, compactLabel, labeled }; Vehicle.currentPowerFigure is the display source
BuildAssessment, BuildSheetExporter, FleetSheet, SpecSheet, VehicleIdentitySurface, GarageSpotlightView, GarageOverviewView render through it
FleetComparisonView POWER column wheel-normalized (currentWheelHorsepowerEstimate) so the shared 'whp' header is true for every row
SpecSheet 'Current' labels measured vs factory and uses the figure's unit
testing_criteria: HonestyInvariantsTests PowerFigure pairing test; HonestySweepTests still green over refactored surfaces

result.notes: View-layer visual checks (spotlight rail, identity surface, comparison grid) still deserve Tim's on-device eyeball — copy lengths changed slightly ('240 HP' where 'XXX WHP' used to render).

items.title: Odometer sanity — entry-time anomaly warnings and a self-contradiction observation
items.why: The odometer is the reasoning spine (currentMileage, milesPerDay, every mileage due state and projection derive from event readings), yet any typo was accepted silently — one slipped digit (50,000 -> 580,000) poisoned all of it with no warning and no surfacing. The review ranked this the biggest unmodeled input risk.
completion_criteria: Vehicle.odometerAnomaly(proposing:on:) flags regressions (below an already-recorded reading of the same era) and implausible rates (> 1,000 mi/day implied), era-aware for backdated entries
AddEditBuildEventView shows a calm, NON-BLOCKING caution next to the mileage field (warn, never restrict the record); editing excludes the event under edit
Steward rule data.odometerRegression surfaces a record that already disagrees with itself (later date, lower reading) as a confirmed caution
testing_criteria: OdometerSanityTests: quiet pass, first-reading pass, regression, slipped digit, road-trip day not flagged, backdated-era consistency, Steward surfacing, consistent record quiet

result.notes: MaintenanceEditorView's baseline field intentionally not gated — it sets a schedule baseline, not a biography reading. The caution never blocks: a genuine correction (replaced cluster, fixing an old typo) must always be loggable.
